rulings.Case Title: RYAN STANOJEV ET AL VS. KEYHAN MOHANNA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 14, 2025 line 8. DEFENDANT JESUS LIM CORP MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD is GRANTED. No opposition filed. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ZHIJUN WU VS. JIANBO ZHONG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Moiton To Vacate Entry Of Default Against Cross-Defendant Zhijun Wu
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 14, 2025 line 1. PLAINTIFF ZHIJUN WU NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOITON TO VACATE ENTRY OF DEFAULT AGAINST CROSS-DEFENDANT ZHIJUN WU is GRANTED. The Default entered on January 17, 2024, based on the First Amended Cross-complaint filed on April 20, 2023, is void. The First Amended Cross-complaint was filed in response to the Court's ruling on March 23, 2023 granting Cross-defendant's Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings with leave to amend. On June 16, 2023, the Court vacated its March 23, 2023 ruling and order associated with the Motion for Judgement on the Pleadings as null and void on the ground that no remittitur was on file at the time of the hearing. Accordingly, the operative cross-complaint in this matter reverts to the initial Cross-complaint filed on December 28, 2021 and answered by cross-defendant on January 5, 2022. The Frist Amended Cross-complaint filed on April 20, 2023 is hereby stricken. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ALBERT H. WONG ET AL VS. THE BANK OF NEW YORK MELLON ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 14, 2025 line 4. DEFENDANT ZBS LAW LLP DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED as to ZBS Law LLP for all causes action, with leave to amend to allege facts showing defects in the Deed and ZBS Law LLP's malice. The dismissal of BONYM's prior lawsuit did not constitute a judicial determination that BONYM was not the holder of the Note. Additionally, the FAC does not overcome ZBS Law's statutory privilege because it does not allege facts that ZBS Law LLP acted with malice. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: KENNETH LITTLE VS. ANTHONY PAUL CARA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 14, 2025 line 5. DEFENDANT CONSUMER DEFENSE LAW GROUP, P.C. (ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS CONSUMER LEGAL DEFENSE GROUP PC), ANTHONY CARA, FERNANDO LEONE MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION is DENIED. Defendants failed to meet their burden of proving by a preponderance of the evidence that the electronic signature on the Retainer Agreement is authentic. (Civ. Code, section1633.9, subd. (a); Evid. Code, section 1401; see also, Ruiz v. Moss Bros. Auto Group, Inc. (2014) 232 Cal.App.4th 836, 843-845; Fabian v. Renovate America, Inc. rulings.Case Title: THE TIDES CENTER VS. BERTA ALONSO ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 14, 2025 line 6. DEFENDANT BERTA ALONSO DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. The complaint states a cause of action for unlawful detainer and is not uncertain. The notice states that it is based on material breach of the lease, not nuisance. The lease permits the landlord to give notice of termination in accordance with all applicable laws. Defendant does not cite any provision of the lease or its attachments which specifically requires the notice to include notice of defendant's rights under the grievance procedure. =(501/SKF) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
